task_description:
# Inconvo Chart Visualization Renderer with Vercel AI SDK

## Background
Inconvo's Vercel AI SDK integration (`@inconvoai/vercel-ai-sdk`) exposes a data agent as a tool that returns structured outputs to the Vercel AI SDK chat stream. When the agent decides the best answer is a chart, the tool output has the shape `{ type: "chart", data: { type: "bar" | "line", x: string[], y: number[], xLabel?: string, yLabel?: string } }`. By default this structured output is not displayed visually — it must be rendered by a React component on the frontend.

A partially-built Next.js application is provided at `/home/user/app`. The scaffold already wires `@inconvoai/vercel-ai-sdk` into `app/api/chat/route.ts` and renders the chat UI in `app/page.tsx` using `useChat` from `@ai-sdk/react`. It also contains a placeholder `app/components/inconvo/InconvoChart.tsx` that currently just returns `null`. The `recharts` package is already installed in `node_modules`.

Your job is to implement the chart-rendering component and wire it into the chat UI so the user sees an actual SVG chart whenever the Inconvo agent returns a chart tool output.

## Requirements
1. Implement `app/components/inconvo/InconvoChart.tsx`:
   - The component must default-export a React component named `InconvoChart`.
   - It accepts a single prop `data` with the shape `{ type: "bar" | "line", x: string[], y: number[], xLabel?: string, yLabel?: string }`.
   - When `data.type === "bar"` it must render a Recharts `<BarChart>` with a `<Bar>` series; for any other `type` it may fall back to a `<BarChart>` as well.
   - The chart must be wrapped in a `<div data-testid="inconvo-chart">` element so it can be selected from the browser DOM.
   - The chart must produce an `<svg>` element (Recharts renders charts as SVG by default).
   - The component must combine `x` and `y` into an array of objects (e.g. `data.x.map((label, i) => ({ name: label, value: data.y[i] }))`) and pass that array to `<BarChart>`. Include `<XAxis dataKey="name" />` and a `<Bar dataKey="value" fill="#8884d8" />`.
   - Mark the file as a client component (`"use client"` at the top).
2. Update `app/page.tsx`:
   - When an Inconvo tool result has `output.type === "chart"`, render `<InconvoChart data={output.data} />` instead of (or in addition to) the existing placeholder rendering.
   - Existing handling for `text` and `table` output types must continue to work.
3. The project must build successfully with `npm run build` and serve successfully with `npm start` on port 3000.

## Implementation Guide
1. Open `/home/user/app/app/components/inconvo/InconvoChart.tsx` and replace the placeholder implementation with a Recharts-based renderer. Import `BarChart`, `Bar`, `XAxis`, `YAxis`, `CartesianGrid`, `Tooltip`, and `ResponsiveContainer` from `recharts`.
2. Map the `{ x: [...], y: [...] }` input shape onto an array of `{ name, value }` objects.
3. Wrap the chart inside a `<div data-testid="inconvo-chart" style={{ width: "100%", height: 320 }}>`.
4. In `app/page.tsx`, locate the branch that handles `part.state === "output-available"` for Inconvo tools and add a case that calls `<InconvoChart data={output.data} />` when `output.type === "chart"`.
5. Run `npm run build` to confirm there are no TypeScript/build errors.
6. Run `npm start` to confirm the app listens on port 3000.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/app`
- Start command: `npm run build && npm start`
- Port: `3000`
- Environment variables: `INCONVO_API_KEY`, `INCONVO_AGENT_ID`, `INCONVO_DB_URL`, and `OPENAI_API_KEY` are set in the environment.
- `recharts` is already installed in `node_modules`; do NOT add or install new dependencies.
- The Inconvo and OpenAI environment variables must be used as-is, never mocked.

## Integrations
- None
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. `cd /home/user/app`
2. `npm run build`
3. `npm start &`
4. Wait for the server to be ready on port 3000.

## Verification Steps
1. Navigate to http://localhost:3000.
2. Verify that a chat input field is visible.
3. Type the message `Show me a bar chart of the top 5 products by total sales` and press Enter.
4. Wait for the assistant to respond.
5. Verify that the DOM contains an element matching `[data-testid="inconvo-chart"]`.
6. Verify that this element contains an `<svg>` element (the Recharts chart).
7. Verify that the rendered chart contains at least 3 `<rect>` bar elements inside the `<svg>` (Recharts renders each `<Bar>` data point as a `<rect>`).

## Specific Test Data
- Chat message: `Show me a bar chart of the top 5 products by total sales`.
- Expected DOM selector: `[data-testid="inconvo-chart"] svg`.

## Expected Results
- The application builds and starts on port 3000.
- After sending the chat message, the page renders an interactive Recharts SVG bar chart inside the `inconvo-chart` test id wrapper, with at least 3 visible bars.

## Integrations
- None
